description: Bandai's Digital Monsters franchise, born in 1997 as a virtual pet for boys. Spans 10 anime series, 19+ films, dozens of console games, and a sprawling mobile graveyard of shuttered gacha titles. Digimon is darker and more serialised than its Pokémon rival — each series stands alone, and the best entries (Tamers, Cyber Sleuth) hold up as genuine sci-fi fiction.

media.title: Digital Monster v-pet (Ver. 1–6 / Pendulum series)
media.description: The original Tamagotchi-style virtual pet that started everything. Six base versions plus the Pendulum, D-3, Digivice, and Xros Loader lines followed over two decades.
media.notes: Physical hardware only. Franchise origin.

media.title: C'mon Digimon (manga one-shot)
media.description: The very first Digimon narrative — a V-Jump one-shot that directly spawned V-Tamer 01 and the broader franchise.
media.notes: Never officially translated to English.

media.title: Digital Monster Ver. S: Digimon Tamers
media.description: The very first Digimon video game — a Sega Saturn fighter/sim exclusive to Japan.
media.notes: Japan exclusive. Emulable via SSF / Kronos.

media.title: Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01 (manga)
media.description: Long-running V-Jump manga (1998–2003) following Taichi and Zeromaru (Veedramon). Different canon from the anime; beloved for crossover chapters with later series.
media.notes: Never officially translated to English. Fan translations available.

media.title: Digimon Adventure
media.description: Seven kids at summer camp are transported to the Digital World and become DigiDestined. The foundational series — introduces Agumon, Gabumon, and the entire original cast.
media.notes: Crunchyroll (sub) / Discotek Blu-ray. Start here.

media.title: Digimon Adventure (1999 film)
media.description: 20-minute short film of Tai and Kari's first encounter with a Digimon four years before the anime. Chronologically the Adventure timeline's true beginning.
media.notes: Mashed into 'Digimon: The Movie' in the west. Watch the Japanese original.

media.title: Digimon World
media.description: Digital pet meets open-world RPG. Raise a single Digimon from baby to mega, repopulate File Island. Demanding and weird but hugely influential.
media.notes: Core: pcsx_rearmed. Also on Steam. Save often — your Digimon dies permanently.

media.title: Digimon World: Digital Card Battle (JP) / Digimon Digital Card Battle (NA)
media.description: Card battler based on the official Digimon CCG. The NA release is actually the enhanced JP sequel.
media.notes: Core: pcsx_rearmed.

media.title: Digimon Adventure: Anode Tamer / Cathode Tamer
media.description: WonderSwan RPGs where Ryo Akiyama's story begins — his arc threads through multiple WS games and ends in the Tamers anime universe.
media.notes: Japan exclusive. WonderSwan emulation limited. Canon Adventure lore.

media.title: Digital Monster Ver. WonderSwan
media.description: WonderSwan port of the original v-pet with isometric battles and 78 Digimon.
media.notes: Japan exclusive.

media.title: Our War Game! (Adventure film 2)
media.description: The DigiDestined fight a viral Digimon attacking the internet in real time. Direct inspiration for the film Summer Wars. Widely considered the best Digimon film.
media.notes: Second segment of western 'Digimon: The Movie'. Watch the Japanese original.

media.title: Digimon Adventure 02: Hurricane Touchdown!! (film)
media.description: T.K. and Kari visit Mimi in New York and encounter Willis, an American DigiDestined, and the corrupted Kokomon. Set during Adventure 02.
media.notes: Third segment of western 'Digimon: The Movie'. Watch the Japanese version.

media.title: Digimon Adventure 3D: Digimon Grand Prix!
media.description: 7-minute CG short screened exclusively at the Sanrio Puroland theme park 2000–2002. Effectively inaccessible — never commercially released.
media.notes: DEFUNCT — theme park exclusive. Never released.

media.title: Digimon: The Movie (western compilation)
media.description: Western theatrical cut splicing three unrelated Japanese short films into 88 minutes with connecting narration. Beloved by nostalgia — a poor representation of the originals.
media.notes: Available on streaming. Watch individual JP films for the real experience.

media.title: Digimon Adventure 02
media.description: Set three years after Adventure. Davis, Yolei, and Cody join T.K. and Kari to fight the Digimon Emperor. Controversial flash-forward epilogue.
media.notes: Direct sequel to Adventure — watch immediately after. Crunchyroll.

media.title: Digimon World 2
media.description: Dungeon-crawler with three tamer factions. Capture up to three Digimon per slot. Grind-heavy sequel to World.
media.notes: Core: pcsx_rearmed.

media.title: Pocket Digimon World (+ Cool & Nature / Wind Battle Disc)
media.description: Three Japan-only PS1 spinoffs: a racing game and two battle disc arena titles. Niche novelties.
media.notes: Japan exclusive. Core: pcsx_rearmed.

media.title: Digimon Adventure 02: Tag Tamers
media.description: Ryo Akiyama partners with Ken Ichijoji (the future Digimon Emperor) in this canonical WonderSwan RPG bridging Adventure 02.
media.notes: Japan exclusive. Canon Adventure lore.

media.title: Digimon Adventure 02: D1 Tamers
media.description: Final WonderSwan RPG in the Ryo arc — the story of how Ryo ends up in the Tamers universe. Canonically important bridge.
media.notes: Japan exclusive. Canon bridge between 02 and Tamers.

media.title: Digimon Adventure 02: Revenge of Diaboromon (film)
media.description: The virus from Our War Game! returns with an army of Kuramon. Tai and Davis lead separate teams. Direct sequel to Our War Game!
media.notes: Available on Crunchyroll.

media.title: Digimon Tamers
media.description: Takato, Henry, and Rika treat Digimon as a card game until their partners become real. Written by Chiaki J. Konaka (Serial Experiments Lain) — the darkest, most sophisticated Digimon series. Wildly acclaimed.
media.notes: Crunchyroll. Best starting point for adult fans. Ryo from 02 WS games appears here. Standalone universe.

media.title: Digimon Tamers: Battle of Adventurers (film)
media.description: Takato visits Okinawa and encounters a Digimon created from a rogue AI. Set during the Tamers series.
media.notes: Canon to Tamers. Available subbed.

media.title: Digimon Battle Spirit (WonderSwan Color / GBA)
media.description: 2D platform fighter featuring Adventure and Tamers Digimon. GBA version is the accessible port.
media.notes: GBA: Core mgba. Japan-only on WSC.

media.title: Digimon Tamers: Digimon Medley
media.description: WonderSwan Color mini-game collection based on the Tamers series.
media.notes: Japan exclusive.

media.title: Digimon Rumble Arena
media.description: Platform fighter with 24 Digimon from the anime. Accessible party game.
media.notes: Core: pcsx_rearmed.

media.title: Digimon Tamers: Pocket Culumon
media.description: Virtual pet mini-game spinoff starring Calumon (Culumon). Japan only.
media.notes: Japan exclusive.

media.title: Digimon Tamers: Brave Tamers
media.description: Final WonderSwan RPG in the Ryo arc — his last adventures before crossing into the Tamers universe. Canonical WS series finale.
media.notes: Japan exclusive. Canon finale to the WonderSwan Ryo storyline.

media.title: Digimon Park
media.description: Japan-only PS1 mini-game collection — guitar, racing, dance. Pure novelty.
media.notes: Japan exclusive.

media.title: Digimon Tamers: Battle Spirit Ver. 1.5
media.description: Updated Battle Spirit with added Tamers characters. Japan-only WonderSwan title.
media.notes: Japan exclusive.

media.title: Digital Monster: D-Project
media.description: Final WonderSwan Digimon title — RPG with digital card game mechanics.
media.notes: Japan exclusive. Last WonderSwan Digimon game.

media.title: Digimon Digital Card Game Ver. WonderSwan Color
media.description: WSC digital adaptation of the Digimon collectible card game.
media.notes: Japan exclusive.

media.title: Digimon Battle Spirit 2
media.description: Sequel fighter featuring Frontier characters. GBA version released in North America.
media.notes: GBA: Core mgba.

media.title: Digimon Tamers: Runaway Locomon (film)
media.description: Epilogue film to Tamers set after the series finale. Rika and the team stop a runaway train Digimon.
media.notes: Canon to Tamers. Subbed version available.

media.title: Digimon World 3
media.description: Three kids get trapped in an online Digimon RPG game. Lighter JRPG with multiple areas. PAL release known as Digimon World 2003.
media.notes: Core: pcsx_rearmed.

media.title: Digimon Frontier
media.description: Takuya and Koji slide-evolve into Digimon themselves using Ancient Spirits. Kids become Digimon rather than partnering with them — divisive but with strong mythology.
media.notes: Crunchyroll. Standalone universe. No partner Digimon mechanic.

media.title: Digimon Frontier: Island of Lost Digimon (film)
media.description: Standalone film with the Frontier cast — a floating island harbours a conflict between human-type and beast-type Digimon.
media.notes: Last Digimon film to receive a western release for many years.

media.title: Digimon Rumble Arena 2
media.description: Expanded sequel fighter with a larger roster drawn from multiple series. The better Rumble Arena game.
media.notes: PS2: PCSX2. GCN: Dolphin. Xbox: Xemu.

media.title: Digimon World 4
media.description: Action RPG spinoff emphasising co-op multiplayer. Digivolve through loot drops. Controversial departure from the series formula.
media.notes: PS2: PCSX2. GCN: Dolphin. Xbox: Xemu.

media.title: Digimon Savers (Digimon Data Squad)
media.description: Marcus Damon and the government DATS organisation defend humanity from rogue Digimon. Older-skewing, fistfight-heavy protagonist. Excellent final arc. Disney-distributed dub is sanitised.
media.notes: Crunchyroll (sub recommended). Standalone universe.

media.title: Digimon Savers 3D / Ultimate Power! Activate Burst Mode!! (film)
media.description: Short film screened at Jump Festa 2007. Marcus and ShineGreymon fight in Burst Mode. ~10 minutes.
media.notes: Never released in English. Jump Festa exclusive screening.

media.title: Digimon World Data Squad
media.description: Turn-based RPG tie-in to the Savers anime. Not part of the main World series despite North American branding.
media.notes: PCSX2 required.

media.title: Digimon Story (Digimon World DS)
media.description: First entry in the Story series — turn-based RPG with a large roster and DigiFarm mechanics. Known as Digimon World DS in the west.
media.notes: Core: melonds.

media.title: Digimon Story: Sunburst / Moonlight (Digimon World Dawn / Dusk)
media.description: Dual-version Story sequel with Light and Dark themed teams. Expanded roster and story.
media.notes: Core: melonds.

media.title: Digimon World Championship
media.description: Virtual pet meets tournament battler. Raise Digimon through the v-pet loop and enter world championship brackets.
media.notes: Core: melonds. Standalone — not Story series despite western branding.

media.title: Digimon Fusion (Digimon Xros Wars)
media.description: Mikey Kudo leads the Fusion Fighters across a fractured Digital World using DigiXros to combine Digimon. Longest series in the franchise at 79 episodes. Season 3 (Young Hunters) was never dubbed in English.
media.notes: Crunchyroll. Season 3 sub-only. Standalone universe.

media.title: Digimon Story: Lost Evolution
media.description: Third Story DS entry — a mystery virus is locking Digimon evolution. Japan only.
media.notes: Japan exclusive. Fan translation exists. Core: melonds.

media.title: Digimon Life (mobile)
media.description: Digimon encyclopedia / library app — 596 unique Digimon pages at shutdown. Shut down March 2012.
media.notes: DEFUNCT — shut down 2012.

media.title: Digimon Collectors (mobile)
media.description: Card-collecting mobile game. Popular in Japan at launch. Servers shut down July 2014.
media.notes: DEFUNCT — servers closed 2014.

media.title: Digimon Story: Super Xros Wars Red / Blue
media.description: Xros Wars anime tie-in dual-version DS games with the DigiXros combining mechanic. Final DS Story titles.
media.notes: Japan exclusive. Fan translation in progress. Core: melonds.

media.title: Digimon World Re:Digitize
media.description: Spiritual successor to Digimon World 1 with modern production values. Returns to the v-pet loop on File Island.
media.notes: Japan exclusive. PPSSPP emulable. Expanded as Decode on 3DS.

media.title: Digimon Adventure (PSP)
media.description: Visual novel + RPG retelling of the Adventure anime. Faithful adaptation with voiced scenes and new artwork.
media.notes: Japan exclusive. PPSSPP emulable. Fan translation patch available.

media.title: Digimon World Re:Digitize Decode
media.description: Enhanced 3DS port of Re:Digitize with additional story chapters. The definitive version. Fan translation available.
media.notes: Japan exclusive. Fan English patch available. Citra/Lime3DS emulable.

media.title: Digimon Crusader / Digimon Heroes! (mobile)
media.description: Card battle mobile RPG featuring many debut Digimon and an original Aegiomon storyline. JP (Crusader) shut down 2014; Global (Heroes!) shut down December 2017.
media.notes: DEFUNCT — JP 2014, Global 2017.

media.title: Digimon All-Star Rumble
media.description: Platform fighter built specifically for western audiences with 12 playable Digimon. Middling reception.
media.notes: PS3: RPCS3. Xbox 360: Xenia.

media.title: Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth
media.description: Aiba investigates the boundary between the real and digital world after being partially digitised. 200+ Digimon, excellent writing, adult cast. The franchise's commercial and critical comeback.
media.notes: On Steam (Complete Edition bundles Hacker's Memory). Best modern entry point.

media.title: Digimon Adventure tri. (6-part film series)
media.description: Six OVA films reuniting the original Adventure cast as older teens to fight corrupted Digimon and the mysterious Meicoomon. Controversial but emotionally resonant. Chapters 1–6 released 2015–2018.
media.notes: Crunchyroll. Watch Adventure + 02 first.

media.title: Digimon Linkz (mobile)
media.description: Farm-building RPG mobile game. Global launch October 2017; shut down July 2019 to make way for ReArise.
media.notes: DEFUNCT — servers closed July 2019.

media.title: Digimon Universe: App Monsters
media.description: Haru Shinkai partners with Gatchmon — an Appmon, a Digimon-adjacent creature born from smartphone apps — to fight the rogue AI Leviathan. Social media and screen addiction themes.
media.notes: Crunchyroll (sub only). Technically its own App Monsters sub-franchise, now defunct. Standalone.

media.title: Digimon World: Next Order
media.description: Spiritual World 1 successor — raise two Digimon simultaneously, rebuild the File Island city. Better pacing than the original but same demanding loop.
media.notes: On Steam and Nintendo Switch. Most accessible modern World game.

media.title: Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth – Hacker's Memory
media.description: Side-story to Cyber Sleuth following hacker Keisuke Amazawa. Parallel timeline with crossover. Expanded roster of 300+ Digimon.
media.notes: On Steam (bundled in Complete Edition). Play Cyber Sleuth first.

media.title: Digimon ReArise (mobile)
media.description: Gacha RPG + visual novel featuring original cast, Herrismon as protagonist's partner, and a layered story about the Spirals phenomenon. Most narrative-rich mobile Digimon game. Shut down April 2022.
media.notes: DEFUNCT — servers closed April 2022. APK exists for offline story.

media.title: Digimon Encounters (mobile)
media.description: Asian-region mobile game. Servers terminated November 2020.
media.notes: DEFUNCT — servers closed November 2020. Asia only.

media.title: Digimon Adventure: (2020 reboot series)
media.description: Full reboot of Adventure set in 2020 with escalated stakes from episode one — the Digital World immediately threatens Earth's infrastructure. Better animation, divisive pacing.
media.notes: Crunchyroll. Standalone reboot — not a sequel to original Adventure.

media.title: Digimon Adventure: Last Evolution Kizuna (film)
media.description: The original DigiDestined face their final battle as young adults, knowing their bond with their Digimon will end when they fully grow up. Emotional closing chapter for the original cast.
media.notes: Crunchyroll. Watch all Adventure, 02, and tri. first.

media.title: Digimon New Century (mobile)
media.description: China/Japan exclusive MMORPG-style mobile game featuring content from all anime series in a crossover setting. Still active in China as of 2025.
media.notes: China/Japan only. VPN required for most users.

media.title: Digimon Ghost Game
media.description: Hiro Amanokawa investigates disturbing 'hologram ghosts' — Digimon manifesting via AR technology. Horror-inflected episodic format riffing on urban legends and body horror. English dub released December 2025.
media.notes: Crunchyroll. English dub (BBC, Dec 2025). Standalone universe.

media.title: Digimon Survive
media.description: Tactical RPG + visual novel hybrid. Students transported to a Digimon world with branching routes, permanent character deaths, and multiple endings. Underrated and emotionally punishing.
media.notes: Steam. ~60% visual novel. Multiple endings require NG+.

media.title: Digimon Super Rumble (mobile / PC)
media.description: Korean-developed MMORPG with global release. Active in some regions as of 2025.
media.notes: Free-to-play MMO. PC client available.

media.title: Digimon Adventure 02: The Beginning (film)
media.description: Feature film set years after 02's epilogue. Rui Owada — the first human to partner with a Digimon — claims primacy over the entire DigiDestined concept.
media.notes: Crunchyroll. Watch Adventure + 02 + Kizuna first.

media.title: Digimon Story: Time Stranger
media.description: Return of the Story JRPG series featuring the Olympos XII as central characters. The modern successor to Cyber Sleuth.
media.notes: Steam (Oct 2025). Latest major Digimon game.

media.title: Digimon Beatbreak
media.description: Tenth anime series focusing on human emotions and a new cast. Began airing October 5, 2025 in Japan.
media.notes: Currently airing. No English dub announced. Crunchyroll expected.

media.title: Digimon Next (manga)
media.description: V-Jump manga by Hamazaki. Tsurugi Tatsuno and his Agumon navigate a world where Digimon materialise via the Battle Terminal arcade system. Ran 2005–2008.
media.notes: Never officially translated to English.

media.title: Digimon Xros Wars (manga)
media.description: Manga adaptation of Xros Wars with significant differences from the TV series — divergent characters and events. Published in English by Viz Media (7 volumes).
media.notes: English release available via Viz.

media.title: Digimon Dreamers (web manga)
media.description: Officially translated English web manga following new tamer Ritsu and Pulsemon. Available online.
media.notes: Officially translated to English. Ongoing as of 2024.
